title: Planning a Picnic
summary: Two friends, Maya and Sophie, make plans for a picnic in the park. They discuss what food and drinks to bring, who will bring what, and what time to meet.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Hey Sophie! The weather forecast says it will be sunny on Saturday. Do you want to go for a picnic?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: That sounds like a great idea! I love picnics. Where should we go?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: I was thinking about Riverside Park. It has a nice open area near the lake and there are some big trees for shade.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Perfect! So what should we bring to eat? We need to plan properly.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: I can make some sandwiches. I'll do cheese and tomato and maybe egg salad. Do you have any food allergies?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: No, I'm fine with everything. I can bring some fruit — I'll get some grapes, strawberries, and maybe a watermelon.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: That's lovely! Should I also bring some crisps and biscuits for snacks?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Yes, definitely. Oh, and we should bring something to drink. I'll get some lemonade and a big bottle of water.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Great. I'll pack the blanket and some paper plates and napkins so we don't have to worry about that.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Good thinking. What time should we meet?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: How about eleven in the morning? That way we can eat lunch there and stay for the afternoon.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Eleven is perfect. Let's meet at the main entrance of the park.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Sounds good! Oh, don't forget to bring sunscreen. It might be very sunny.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Good point! I'll bring my sunhat too. I'm really looking forward to it. See you Saturday!

annotations.exampleEn: According to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow afternoon.

annotations.meaningVi: bóng mát (nơi không có ánh nắng trực tiếp)
annotations.exampleEn: We sat under the tree to enjoy the shade.
He opened a bag of crisps and shared them with his friends.
She made a jug of fresh lemonade for the picnic.
They spread a blanket on the grass and sat down.
She put some napkins on the table for the guests.
We agreed to meet at the main entrance of the museum.
Don't forget to apply sunscreen before going to the beach.
I'm really looking forward to the holidays next week.

annotations.meaningVi: ý kiến hay, nghĩ đúng rồi
annotations.exampleEn: 'I brought an umbrella just in case.' 'Good thinking!'

grammarNotes.title: 'I'll' for spontaneous decisions and offers
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I'll (I will) + động từ nguyên thể' được dùng khi bạn đưa ra quyết định ngay tại thời điểm nói, không phải là kế hoạch đã có từ trước. Ví dụ: 'I'll do cheese and tomato.' — Tôi sẽ làm bánh mì kẹp phô mai và cà chua. 'I'll get some grapes.' — Tôi sẽ mua nho. Đây là cách rất phổ biến khi phân công công việc hoặc đưa ra đề nghị giúp đỡ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'I'll + infinitive' for spontaneous decisions made at the moment of speaking, and for offers to do something. Examples: 'I'll make some sandwiches.' / 'I'll pack the blanket.' These decisions are made during the conversation, not planned beforehand. Contrast with 'I'm going to + infinitive' which describes a pre-planned action: 'I'm going to buy fruit tomorrow' (already planned).

grammarNotes.title: Making suggestions: 'How about...?' and 'Should we...?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'How about + noun/gerund (-ing)?' và 'Should we + động từ nguyên thể?' là hai cách phổ biến để đề xuất ý kiến. Ví dụ: 'How about eleven in the morning?' — Mười một giờ sáng thì sao? 'Should I also bring some crisps?' — Tôi có nên mang thêm khoai tây chiên không? 'What should we bring?' — Chúng ta nên mang gì? Khi đồng ý với đề xuất, ta có thể nói 'That sounds great!', 'Perfect!', hoặc 'That's lovely!'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Two common ways to make suggestions: (1) 'How about + noun or -ing verb?' — 'How about eleven in the morning?' (2) 'Should we/I + bare infinitive?' — 'Should I also bring some crisps?' To accept a suggestion, use: 'That sounds great!', 'Perfect!', 'Definitely.' To decline politely: 'Actually, I think...' or 'Maybe we could...' instead.

translationVi:
Maya: Này Sophie! Dự báo thời tiết nói thứ bảy sẽ nắng. Bạn có muốn đi dã ngoại không?
Sophie: Nghe hay quá! Tôi thích dã ngoại lắm. Chúng ta nên đến đâu?
Maya: Tôi đang nghĩ đến Công viên Riverside. Nơi đó có khu mở rộng đẹp gần hồ và có vài cây lớn để có bóng mát.
Sophie: Hoàn hảo! Vậy chúng ta nên mang gì để ăn? Chúng ta cần lên kế hoạch đàng hoàng.
Maya: Tôi có thể làm bánh mì kẹp. Tôi sẽ làm phô mai và cà chua và có thể salad trứng. Bạn có dị ứng thực phẩm không?
Sophie: Không, tôi ổn với mọi thứ. Tôi có thể mang trái cây — tôi sẽ mua nho, dâu tây và có thể một quả dưa hấu.
Maya: Thích quá! Tôi có nên mang thêm khoai tây chiên và bánh quy ăn vặt không?
Sophie: Có, nhất định rồi. À, và chúng ta nên mang gì đó để uống. Tôi sẽ mang nước chanh và một chai nước lớn.
Maya: Tốt rồi. Tôi sẽ gói chăn và một số đĩa giấy và khăn ăn để chúng ta không phải lo về điều đó.
Sophie: Hay đấy. Chúng ta nên gặp nhau lúc mấy giờ?
Maya: Mười một giờ sáng thì sao? Như vậy chúng ta có thể ăn trưa ở đó và ở lại đến chiều.
Sophie: Mười một giờ là hoàn hảo. Chúng ta gặp nhau ở lối vào chính của công viên nhé.
Maya: Nghe ổn! À, đừng quên mang kem chống nắng. Trời có thể nắng rất to đấy.
Sophie: Ý kiến hay! Tôi cũng sẽ mang nón rộng vành. Tôi thực sự mong chờ điều này. Hẹn gặp thứ bảy!

questions.prompt: What does the weather forecast say about Saturday?
questions.explanation: Maya says 'The weather forecast says it will be sunny on Saturday.'
questions.hint: Maya mentions the forecast at the start.

questions.prompt: Which park do they decide to go to?
questions.hint: Maya suggests a park near water.

questions.prompt: What feature of the park does Maya mention for shade?
questions.explanation: 'there are some big trees for shade'
questions.hint: It is a natural feature of the park.

options: Cheese and tomato, and egg salad
Chicken and lettuce, and egg salad
questions.explanation: 'I'll do cheese and tomato and maybe egg salad.'
questions.hint: She mentions two specific filling options.

questions.prompt: Does Sophie have any food allergies?
options: Yes, she is allergic to eggs
Yes, she is allergic to strawberries
No, she is fine with everything

questions.prompt: Which fruit does Sophie NOT mention bringing?
questions.explanation: Sophie mentions grapes, strawberries, and watermelon. Mangoes are not mentioned.
questions.hint: Three fruits are listed; one option was not said.

options: Lemonade and a big bottle of water
questions.explanation: 'I'll get some lemonade and a big bottle of water.'

questions.prompt: What will Maya pack apart from food?
options: A tent and a folding table
A blanket, paper plates, and napkins
questions.explanation: 'I'll pack the blanket and some paper plates and napkins.'

questions.prompt: What time do they agree to meet?
questions.hint: Maya makes the suggestion and Sophie agrees.

options: At the main entrance of the park
questions.explanation: 'Let's meet at the main entrance of the park.'
questions.hint: Sophie suggests a specific meeting point.

questions.prompt: What does Maya remind Sophie not to forget?
questions.hint: Maya gives a reminder related to the sunny weather.

questions.prompt: What extra item will Sophie bring for the sun?
questions.hint: She adds it to the sunscreen reminder.

cloze.template:
Maya: The weather {{b1}} says it will be sunny on Saturday. Do you want to go for a picnic?
Sophie: That sounds like a great idea! Where should we go?
Maya: I was thinking about Riverside Park. There are some big trees for {{b2}}.
Sophie: Perfect! What should we bring to {{b3}}?
Maya: I can make some {{b4}}. Do you have any food {{b5}}?
Sophie: No, I'm fine with everything. I can bring some {{b6}} — grapes, strawberries, and maybe a watermelon.
Maya: Should I also bring some crisps and {{b7}} for snacks?
Sophie: Yes, definitely. And I'll get some {{b8}} and a big bottle of water to drink.
Maya: I'll pack the {{b9}} and some paper plates and napkins.
Sophie: What time should we meet?
Maya: How about {{b10}} in the morning?
Sophie: Perfect. Let's meet at the main {{b11}} of the park.
Maya: Don't forget to bring {{b12}}. It might be very sunny.

blanks.explanation: 'forecast' — a weather forecast is a prediction of future weather.
'shade' — shade is the cool, dark area protected from direct sunlight.
'eat' — Sophie asks about food for the picnic.
'sandwiches' — Maya says she will make sandwiches with different fillings.
'allergies' — a food allergy is a reaction to a specific food.
'fruit' — Sophie offers to bring grapes, strawberries, and watermelon.
'biscuits' — Maya suggests crisps and biscuits as snacks.
'lemonade' — Sophie says she will get lemonade and water.
'blanket' — a picnic blanket is spread on the grass to sit on.
'eleven' — Maya suggests eleven in the morning and Sophie agrees.
'entrance' — the main entrance is the primary way into the park.
'sunscreen' — Maya reminds Sophie to bring sunscreen for the sunny day.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Maya and Sophie plan a picnic in the park together. Spending time outdoors with friends is important for many people. Do you prefer to spend your free time indoors or outdoors, and why? What are the benefits of spending time in nature?
